revision.text:
[[ja:ヒロ大神宮]] {{infobox religious building |name = Hilo Daijingu |native_name = ヒロ大神宮
Hilo Daijingu |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = File:Hilo Daijingu.jpg |address = 10 Anela St. Hilo, HI 96720 USA |location = {{coord|19|41|38.5|N|155|4|42.9|W|display=inline,title|region:US_type:landmark}} |map = |enshrined_deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} |shrine_status = |established = {{ill|1898|en|1898|qid=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}}（{{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}31） |main_hall = |alternative_names = |signboards = |annual_festival = |rituals = |location = {{ill|Hawaii County|qid=Q487334|1=Hawaiʻi County, Hawaii}} |coordinates = {{Coord|19.694028|N|-155.078583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Hilo Daijingu 2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Hilo Daijingu's {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080012}}]] '''Hilo Daijingu''' (ヒロ大神宮) is a Shinto shrine ({{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|qid=Q11590310|lt=Shimmei Shrine|1=Shinmei shrines}})) located in {{ill|United States|en|United States|qid=Q30|lt=United States|1=United States}}, {{ill|Hawaii|en|Hawaii|qid=Q782|lt=Hawaii|1=Hawaii}}, on [[:en:Hawaii (island)|Hawaii Island]], in {{ill|Hilo (Hawaii Island)|ja|ヒロ (ハワイ島)|lt=Hilo|lt_ja=ヒロ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hilo (Hawaii Island)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒロ (ハワイ州)|1=Hilo, Hawaii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q216258}}. Its former name was '''Yamato Shrine''' (大和神社). It is the oldest shrine outside {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.[http://tokuhain.arukikata.co.jp/hawaii_island/2014/11/post_16.html The Earth's Walk HP (Traveler’s Guide TOP > Correspondent Blog > Hawaii > Hawaii Island Correspondent Blog > Visiting the Oldest Existing Shrine Overseas...)] It is the chief guardian shrine of Hawaii Island. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deities|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deities|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} == History == * November 3, {{ill|1898|en|1898|qid=Q7848|lt=1898|1=1898}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 31), founded by the first {{ill|Head Priest|ja|宮司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head Priest|1=Gūji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453500}}, 合志覚太. == Address == * 10 Anela St. Hilo, HI 96720 USA == See Also == * {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} * {{ill|Daijingu Temple of Hawaii|en|Daijingu Temple of Hawaii|qid=Q11327031|lt=Hawaii Daijingu|1=Daijingu Temple of Hawaii}} * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in the United States|en|List of Shinto shrines in the United States|qid=Q15932564|lt=List of Shinto Shrines in the United States|1=List of Shinto shrines in the United States}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official website|https://hilodaijingu.amebaownd.com/|HILO DAIJINGU}} Hilo Daijingu Official Website * [http://himoji.jp/database/db04/preview.php?name=%E3%83%92%E3%83%AD%E5%A4%A7%E7%A5%9E%E5%AE%AE Hilo Daijingu (Yamato Shrine)] - {{ill|Kanagawa University|en|Kanagawa University|qid=Q203624|lt=Kanagawa University|1=Kanagawa University}} Non-text Materials Research Center's Database on Overseas Shrines (Ruins) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|ヒロ大神宮|version=87965974|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:ヒロ大神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q24872796}} [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the United States]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
